questions.q: In which Indian city are the Qutb Shahi Tombs located?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Shahi Tombs lie in Hyderabad, near the Golconda Fort.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state are the Qutb Shahi Tombs situated?
questions.explanation: Hyderabad, where the tombs stand, is the capital of the state of Telangana.

questions.q: What kind of site are the Qutb Shahi Tombs?
questions.explanation: The site is a necropolis containing the domed tombs of the Qutb Shahi rulers and their family.

questions.q: Whose tombs make up this complex?
questions.explanation: The complex holds the tombs of the sultans of the Qutb Shahi dynasty that ruled the Golconda kingdom.

questions.q: Near which famous fort are the Qutb Shahi Tombs located?
questions.explanation: The tombs stand a short distance from Golconda Fort, the dynasty's stronghold.

questions.q: What architectural feature crowns most of the Qutb Shahi tombs?
questions.explanation: Each major tomb is topped by a dome, typically raised on a square or octagonal base.

questions.q: The Qutb Shahi Tombs are set within what kind of surroundings?
questions.explanation: The tombs are laid out within gardens, in keeping with the garden-tomb tradition.

questions.q: The Qutb Shahi sultans were the rulers of which kingdom?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Shahi dynasty ruled the Golconda Sultanate from the early 16th to the late 17th century.

questions.q: The religious affiliation reflected in the design of the Qutb Shahi Tombs is primarily which?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Shahis were Muslim rulers, and their tombs follow Islamic mausoleum traditions.

questions.q: Roughly which centuries saw the building of the Qutb Shahi Tombs?
questions.explanation: The tombs were raised over the lifespan of the Qutb Shahi dynasty, mainly in the 16th and 17th centuries.

questions.q: The Qutb Shahi Tombs blend Persian and Pathan styles with elements drawn from which Indian tradition?
questions.explanation: The tombs combine Persian and Pathan forms with local Hindu architectural elements.

questions.q: The founder of the Qutb Shahi dynasty, whose line is buried here, was which ruler?
questions.explanation: Sultan Quli Qutb-ul-Mulk founded the dynasty, and his tomb is among the earliest in the necropolis.

questions.q: Which Qutb Shahi ruler, buried here, is credited with founding the city of Hyderabad and the Charminar?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Quli Qutb Shah, who founded Hyderabad and built the Charminar in 1591, is buried in the complex.

questions.q: The tombs are generally built on which kind of base plan beneath the dome?
questions.explanation: The mausoleums typically rise from a square or octagonal platform supporting the dome.

questions.q: What was the principal building material used for the Qutb Shahi Tombs?
questions.explanation: The tombs were built of local grey granite and masonry, often finished with stucco plaster and once richly decorated.

questions.q: Besides the sultans, who else are buried in the Qutb Shahi necropolis?
options: Members of the royal family and court figures
questions.explanation: The complex holds tombs of queens, princes, royal relatives and notable court figures alongside the sultans.

questions.q: Decorative glazed tiles in colours such as blue and green that once adorned the tombs reflect the influence of which artistic tradition?
questions.explanation: The tombs once bore Persian-style glazed tilework in blues and greens, traces of which survive.

questions.q: Many of the tombs are surrounded by an open arcaded gallery on the lower level. What is the chief purpose of these arched verandahs?
options: To form a colonnaded ambulatory around the burial chamber
questions.explanation: Arched verandahs form a gallery encircling the central burial chamber on the lower storey of the larger tombs.

questions.q: The Qutb Shahi rule over Golconda came to an end in 1687 when the kingdom was annexed by which power?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb's Mughal forces captured Golconda in 1687, ending the Qutb Shahi dynasty.

questions.q: Within the complex there is a mortuary structure where bodies were ritually washed before burial. What is such a structure commonly called?
questions.explanation: The complex includes a mortuary bath (hammam) used for the ritual washing of the dead before burial.

questions.q: Why are the Qutb Shahi Tombs historically significant as a group?
options: They are India's oldest Buddhist site
They were the seat of the Mughal court
They form one of the few necropolises with successive rulers of a single dynasty buried together
They contain the tomb of a Mughal emperor
questions.explanation: The site is notable for gathering nearly all the rulers of one dynasty in a single dynastic burial ground.

questions.q: What is the present official conservation status of the Qutb Shahi Tombs?
options: A UNESCO World Heritage Site since the 1980s
An unprotected ruin with no legal status
A protected heritage site under major conservation, on India's tentative UNESCO list
questions.explanation: The tombs are a protected heritage complex undergoing extensive conservation and are on India's UNESCO tentative list, not yet inscribed.

questions.q: The last sultan of the Qutb Shahi dynasty, deposed by Aurangzeb in 1687, was which ruler?
questions.explanation: Abul Hasan, known as Tana Shah, was the last Qutb Shahi sultan, defeated and imprisoned by Aurangzeb in 1687.

questions.q: Because the last sultan died a Mughal prisoner far from Golconda, what is unusual about the dynastic burials at the complex?
options: Every sultan without exception is buried here
The last sultan, Abul Hasan, is not buried in the complex
No sultan is actually buried here
Only the last sultan is buried here
questions.explanation: Abul Hasan Tana Shah died in Mughal captivity and was not interred at the necropolis, unlike his predecessors.

questions.q: The official language and literary culture promoted by the Qutb Shahis, reflected in the inscriptions on their tombs, was strongly associated with which language?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Shahis were great patrons of Persian and Deccani Urdu, languages reflected in the calligraphic inscriptions on the tombs.

questions.q: The dynasty whose tombs these are originally came to the Deccan as nobles serving which earlier Deccan kingdom?
questions.explanation: Sultan Quli first served the Bahmani Sultanate before establishing the independent Qutb Shahi kingdom of Golconda.

questions.q: What does the Persian-derived word 'shahi' in the name 'Qutb Shahi' broadly signify?
questions.explanation: 'Shahi' derives from 'shah' (king), conveying the royal character of the dynasty and its tombs.

questions.q: The tombs originally featured tall ornamental finials and parapet decorations that have largely been lost. Such projecting crowning ornaments around a dome's base are part of what feature?
options: The decorative parapet and finial crown of the dome
questions.explanation: Ornamental parapets and finials once crowned the domes, much of which decoration has eroded over the centuries.

questions.q: Large-scale modern conservation of the Qutb Shahi Tombs has been carried out in partnership with which organization?
options: The Aga Khan Trust for Culture
questions.explanation: The Aga Khan Trust for Culture has led a major conservation and restoration effort at the Qutb Shahi necropolis.

questions.q: The Qutb Shahi Tombs are widely regarded as a key surviving example of which broader regional architectural tradition?
questions.explanation: The complex is a defining example of Deccani Indo-Islamic architecture developed under the Qutb Shahis.
